Off Calendar Jul-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFFS LEW JENKINS AND LINDA M. JENKINS, AS TRUSTEES OF THE JENKINS TRUST U/A/D 1/15/98 AND JACK H. FRESKOS, BENEFICIARY OF IRA 042640 AT WESTAMERICA MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO PORTIONS OF DEFENDANTS THE PROPERTY SCIENCES GROUP, BERNARD A. DUER, PAUL E. CHANDLER, AND JUSTIN EICHMAN'S ANSWER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Plaintiff's motion for Judgment on the Pleadings as to Defenses 3 (contrib. negl.), 5 (other's comparative fault) and 23 (assumption of risk) is Granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show plaintiff's alleged irrational conduct. Plaintiff's motion as to Defense 7 (no reasonable reliance) is granted without leave to amend as this is not an affirmative defense but raises an element of Plaintiff's Prima Facie case on the claim of alleged misrepresentation. Defendant's denial of the allegations in the complaint put Plaintiff's reasonable reliance at issue. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-12-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS THE PROPERTIES SCIENCES GROUP, INC, BERNARD DUER, AND PAUL CHANDLER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Appearances:
Eric T. Hartnett of the Law Offices of Peter N. Brewer (650.327.2900 x14) appearing for plaintiff and responding party
Peter C. Catalanotti of Manning & Marder (217.6990) appearing for defendants and moving parties
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Sustain demurrer to second cause of action with leave to amend to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for negligent misrepresentation. Sustain demurrer to third cause of action with leave to amend to plead with more specificity which prong(s) of Unfair Competition Law apply, and with factual allegations. Order signed in open Court. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
